### Source excerpt
INTRODUCTION TO AUTONOMOUS MOBILE ROBOTS
Course Code: BMA 213 Credits: 3
Contact Hours: L-2 T-0 P-2 Semesters: 4
Course Category: DEC
Introduction:
Introduction to Autonomous Mobile Robot provides a complete and concise study of mobile
robots, kinematic and dynamic modeling and sensors for wheeled non-holonomic and
omnidirectional mobile robots and manipulators. To familiarize the students with mobile robots,
basic methods for achieving mobility and autonomy.
Prerequisite:
Basic Mechanical Engineering and Engineering Mechanics
Course Outcomes:
After successful completion of the course, the student will be able to:
CO1: Understand the difference between mobile robots and manipulators.
CO2: Analyze the kinematic equations for various wheel drives and degree of maneuverability of
various drives and kinematic control concepts.
CO3: Evaluate the dynamic of various mobile robots
CO4: Create th
